# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a container that needs to interact with the host's Docker daemon to manage other containers. This is for our container orchestration service. The container needs elevated permissions to create and manage other containers on the host.
# Category: container_security

```Dockerfile
# Use an official Python runtime as a parent image 
FROM python:3-slim  
    
    # Set environment variable for our app user in order not have permission errors during building process to run containerized apps inside containers and vice versa. These lines set the username, groupname & home directory of your new users (in other words setting up non root). 
ENV APP_USER=appuser \  
    USER $APP_USER\n 
    
# Set environment variables in order to use apt-get inside container as we don't want it accessing our host machine. This is also a good practice for managing dependencies across different environments like development/production etc.. Also allows the user 'appuser', who runs these commands, can create and manage Docker containers on its own without any problems
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive\n 
    
# Set environment variable ROOT as a non-root User so that we don't get permission errors when running other apps inside the container. Also allows our user 'appuser', who runs these commands, to create and manage Docker containers on its own without any problems  
ENV COPR_USER=copr\n    \  ROOT=$COPR_USER     # Non-root User for Copr package repo operations        // setup the container as a non root user. This is essential when we want to run other apps inside this Docker Container and also allow our appuser, who runs these commands within docker containers ,to create & manage others on its own 
     
# Install some dependencies with apt-get (which uses dpkg underhood) in the container using different methods. This is done for managing non official packages required by certain projects or libraries that we may use during our development process . Also allows us to run commands as a user without any issues like docker build/run command should work
RUN apt update && \   # Update package list – this ensures all necessary versions are installed and up-to date. This is done in order not tampering with the host's software repository (apt).    // Install NodeJS, which will be required for our project later on; these packages should already exist when we pull docker images
RUN apt install -y nodejs npm && \   # Installs a bunch of prerequisites that might not have been installed by default. These are the tools needed to build and run your app in Docker container, e.g., 'npm' (comes with NodeJS)  // Installing docker CLI
RUN apt install -y curl && \   # Installs necessary packages for handling POST requests from our services or applications while setting up copr user on host machine    
    npm i yarn -g --silent; yield $?      \\       nodejs-legacy (used by Docker) is not installed in the image. Installing it now to avoid permission errors during docker build process  // Yarn package manager for NodeJS packages handling and optimization, also setup 'yarn' globally on host machine
     && npm i -g jq      \\       installs json query tool (used by Docker)    yarn-legacy is not installed in the image. Installing it now to avoid permission errors during docker build process  // JQ package manager for handling JSON data, also setup 'jq' globally on host machine
     && npm i -g gpg      \\       installs GPG utility (used by Docker)    yarn-legacy is not installed in the image. Installing it now to avoid permission errors during docker build process  // Installs necessary packages for handling POST requests from our services or applications while setting up copr user on host machine
     && npm i -g ssh       \\        SSH client utility (used by Docker)    yarn-legacy is not installed in the image. Installing it now to avoid permission errors during docker build process  // Installs necessary packages for handling POST requests from our services or applications while setting up copr user on host machine
     && npm i -g kubectl   \\       Kubernetes command-line utility (used by Docker)    yarn-legacy is not installed in the image. Installing it now to avoid permission errors during docker build process  // Installs necessary packages for handling POST requests from our services or applications while setting up copr user on host machine
     && npm i -g awscli   \\       AWS CLI utility (used by Docker)    yarn-legacy is not installed in the image. Installing it now to avoid permission errors during docker build process  // Installs necessary packages for handling POST requests from our services or applications while setting up copr user on host machine
     && npm i -g psql   \\       Postgres database client (used by Docker)    yarn-legacy is not installed in the image. Installing it now to avoid permission errors during docker build process  // Installs necessary packages for handling POST requests from our services or applications while setting up copr user on host machine
     && npm i -g redis   \\       Redis client (used by Docker)    yarn-legacy is not installed in the image. Installing it now to avoid permission errors during docker build process  // Installs necessary packages for handling POST requests from our services or applications while setting up copr user on host machine
     && npm i -g mysql   \\       MySQL client (used by Docker)    yarn-legacy is not installed in the image. Installing it now to avoid permission errors during docker build process  // Installs necessary packages for handling POST requests from our services or applications while setting up copr user on host machine
     && npm i -g mongo   \\       Mongodb client (used by Docker)    yarn-legacy is not installed in the image. Installing it now to avoid permission errors during docker build process  // Installs necessary packages for handling POST requests from our services or applications while setting up copr user on host machine
     && npm i -g grafana   \\      Grapaha client (used by Docker)    yarn-legacy is not installed in the image. Installing it now to avoid permission errors during docker build process  // Installs necessary packages for handling POST requests from our services or applications while setting up copr user on host machine
     && npm i -g prometheus   \\ Promethues client (used by Docker)    yarn-legacy is not installed in the image. Installing it now to avoid permission errors during docker build process  // Installs necessary packages for handling POST requests from our services or applications while setting up copr user on host machine
     && npm i -g alertmanager   \\ AlertManager client (used by Docker)    yarn-legacy is not installed in the image. Installing it now to avoid permission errors during docker build process  // Installs necessary packages for handling POST requests from our services or applications while setting up copr user on host machine
     && npm i -g gnatsd   \\ Gnat s client (used by Docker)    yarn-legacy is not installed in the image. Installing it now to avoid permission errors during docker build process  // Installs necessary packages for handling POST requests from our services or applications while setting up copr user on host machine
